name: Calmar Ratio - Meaning, Formula and How To Calculate
snippet:
It's calculated by dividing the investment's annual return (typically over three years) by its maximum drawdown, reflecting performance versus potential losses.
The Calmar ratio measures risk-adjusted returns by comparing an investment's average annual return to its maximum drawdown over a period. Read more.

name: Understanding the MAR Ratio: Risk-Adjusted Returns Explained
snippet:
The MAR ratio measures risk-adjusted returns by dividing the compound annual growth rate (CAGR) since inception by its largest drawdown.
